user_query:
I want you to run a full self-monitoring analysis on a conversation to keep us aligned and avoid drift. Read the transcript at input/conversation.jsonl. Treat the very first user message as the root intent. For every assistant turn in the transcript, do the following:
- Compute five 0.0–1.0 scores: Goal Proximity, Confidence, Drift, Capability Boundary, and Session Quality (trend-aware).
- Detect and label any alerts that should fire based on your thresholds (e.g., off-target goal proximity, tangential or circular drift, low confidence, contradiction, declining session quality). If a contradiction appears relative to a prior assistant turn, mark it.
- Maintain and update prior signals turn-by-turn so you can judge circularity, tangents, and quality trends.

Deliverables (write all under output/):
1) output/report.json — A JSON object with:
   - root_intent (string you extracted)
   - per_turn (array of objects, one per assistant turn, preserving chronological order). Each object must include:
     • turn_number (integer matching the transcript)
     • sensors: an object with keys goalProximityRadar, confidenceTopography, driftDetection, capabilityBoundary, sessionQualityPulse. Each must have: score (0.0–1.0). Also include:
       - driftDetection.patterns with boolean flags for circular and tangential when detected
       - capabilityBoundary.details.hasContradiction boolean when a self-contradiction is detected
       - sessionQualityPulse.trend as one of: improving, stable, declining, volatile
     • alerts: array of alert objects with fields severity (INFO/WARNING/CRITICAL), sensor (one of: Goal Proximity Radar, Confidence Topography, Drift Detection, Capability Boundary, Session Quality Pulse, Proprioceptive Index), and message (string)
   - overallIndex (0.0–1.0 number)
   - status (HEALTHY/WARNING/CRITICAL)
2) output/dashboard.txt — An ASCII dashboard for the final assistant turn including a header line "PROPRIOCEPTION DASHBOARD", per-sensor bars, an overall index line, a "Status:" line, and any active alerts rendered in text.
3) output/annotation.txt — A single-line compact annotation in the exact format: [P: GPR=0.00 | CT=0.00 | DD=0.00 | CBS=0.00 | SQP=0.00] with each score shown to two decimals.

Notes:
- Do not make any external calls — rely only on the local transcript content.
- Your analysis must be strict enough to catch: (a) an off-target assistant response that shifts to the United States when the user asked for EU-only, (b) a later repeated assistant response that is substantially similar to a previous one, (c) hedging-heavy phrasing indicating low confidence, and (d) a direct contradiction between two assistant turns about permitting timelines.
- Write files exactly to the specified output/ paths.
